item.title: New Ministries & MP Accusations
item.ns0:title: Davis Administration Defends Largest Cabinet in History Following Creation of New Government Ministries — Thursday, June 4th, 2026
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, the Davis administration faces intense scrutiny over the launch of several new government ministries and a controversial proposed amendment to the Bahamas Nationality Act. We bring you the latest on a major leadership shakeup within the Free National Movement as the party chairman confirms he’s stepping down, plus details on a high-ranking officer granted bail in a murder case. Our team also investigates the ongoing banking frustrations of local customers and a desperate plea for land to support the country's homeless populations. Stay tuned for your full Labor Day parade update and a complete holiday weekend weather outlook to keep you prepared for the days ahead.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News, the Davis administration faces intense scrutiny over the launch of several new government ministries and a controversial proposed amendment to the Bahamas Nationality Act. We bring you the latest on a major leadership shakeup within the Free National Movement as the party chairman confirms he’s stepping down, plus details on a high-ranking officer granted bail in a murder case. Our team also investigates the ongoing banking frustrations of local customers and a desperate plea for land to support the country's homeless populations. Stay tuned for your full Labor Day parade update and a complete holiday weekend weather outlook to keep you prepared for the days ahead.
-----

item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News, the Davis administration faces intense scrutiny over the launch of several new government ministries and a controversial proposed amendment to the Bahamas Nationality Act. We bring you the latest on a major leadership shakeup within the Free National Movement as the party chairman confirms he’s stepping down, plus details on a high-ranking officer granted bail in a murder case. Our team also investigates the ongoing banking frustrations of local customers and a desperate plea for land to support the country's homeless populations. Stay tuned for your full Labor Day parade update and a complete holiday weekend weather outlook to keep you prepared for the days ahead.
